Radhanath Swami Transcriptions Template

Meta Data v9

Event Name/Title

How to be Krishna Conscious

Event Date

28 Oct 1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai, ISKCON Chowpatty

Event Type

Other

Transcriber

Gaurav Kansara

Completed

08 Apr 2022

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-079%20Q&A%20How%20To%20Be%20Krishna%20Conscious%20(Mumbai)-QA-dt_89_10_28.mp3

Source Video

Verse

Click here to enter text.

Tags – Places

Badrikashram, Venkateshwara temple, Tirupati

Tags – Pastimes

Marriage of Laxmi devi and the debt of Venkateshwara, Raslila.

Tags – Persons

Maharaj Parikshit, Sukhdev Goswami, Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati, Srinivas, Prahlada Maharaja, Gopis.

Tags – Themes

Fasting, Shaving of head, Visiting holy places with a devotee, Everything belongs to Krishna, reading and hearing, the desire to love Krishna, bringing up children in Krishna consciousness

Q&A Transcription

[devotee:] So anybody has any questions you can ask Maharaj. Before you gave, you were asking me - what is the meaning of fasting? Why we…why do we fast? On the particular day, why do we fast?

The purpose of fasting

[Maharaja:] Fasting is performed for several purposes. The most important reason, for any spiritual discipline is - to increase our condition…the facility of our condition to meditate and remem...upon and remember Krishna. In other words, when we reduce our bodily necessities, we have more time to completely centre all of our energies in the remembrance of Krishna. Therefore, the essence of fasting is, that by minimizing the time we take to eat, the energy of our body to digest foods for our own maintenance - we utilize every drop of that energy to immerse our thought in Krishna.

Traditionally on fast days, you do not work either. Traditionally on a fast day, you do nothing except Japa and Puja; and Sadhu Satsang and nothing else. Just like Maharaja Parikshit, he made a vow to fast till death. When he was given the notice that he had on…only seven days to live. Now why did he vow to fast till death. Not simply for the sake of fasting, but so that he had absolutely nothing else, to distract his attention away from hearing the glories of the lord from Sukhdev Goswami. In other words, he wanted every drop of his mental and physical energy to be going into the remembrance of Krishna.

So if you're fasting and you're doing all nonsense while you're fasting, engaging in so many worldly activities, the real benefits of the fast..fast are minimal. The real purpose is to redirect all of our energies to that process by which we can remember Krishna.

Just like Ekadashi is a day when people fast. The disciples of his Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada, they chant sixteen rounds a day minimum. Prabhupada said, “If you fast on Ekadashi, you should chant at least twenty-five rounds every day…on that day. Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati, his disciples - they would fast every day on Ekadashi and they would chant at least sixty four rounds every day, or more. So the real purpose of everything we do is to fix our mind on Krishna; and fasting is a very very effective means of assisting us in fixing our mind exclusively on the name and the form and the pastimes of the lord.

Another reason for fasting is health. Fasting is very very good for the health; and by fasting regularly, our body is in better physical condition to render service to the lord. So if we're fasting for a better body, physical condition, for the purpose of increasing our service, that is also a spiritual pursuit.

Another reason for fasting is to increase our compassion [break] especially for rich people. We do not…we do not generally sympathize with the suffering of another, unless you have suffered like that. Like oftentimes a person, who is born in a very poor family, who becomes very rich, that person is more inclined toward being very philanthropic to the poor, than someone who has simply brought up in a rich family. Because he…he has experienced in his own life, the sufferings of the poor. So therefore poor people are hungry, often; and a rich man, in order for him to really have a sense of compassion for the poor, he should understand and know what hunger is like. Therefore, by fasting you have a sense of the suffering of hunger pangs. And then when someone approaches you, who is hungry, you will be very very charitable, and very compassionate; because you would feel for him, you have been through it.

Another reason is for the economic purpose of maintaining a high supply of foodstuffs for the whole society. If a society is oriented in such a way where everyone fasts, two days a month, from grains. Grains are the staple food for everyone. That means, how much more of a surplus of grains it will be for everyone; if everyone does not eat grains for two days a month. And it will strengthen the economic position, especially in…in a…village environment. So this is one of the reasons people would fast.

But as devotees we should understand that the most important and most essential reason, why, if we do anything, is for the purification of our heart. Now the purification of our heart does not take place simply from refraining from food, but it takes place by the remembrance of Krishna.

om apavitraḥ pavitra vā

sarvāvasthāṁ gato 'pi vā

yaḥ smaret puṇḍarīkākṣaṁ

sa bahyābhyantaraḥ śuciḥ

[Garuḍa Purāṇa]

[“Whether one is pure or contaminated, and regardless of one’s external situation, simply by remembering the lotus-eyed Personality of Godhead one can internally and externally cleanse one’s existence.”]

Whether one is impure or pure, if you remember the beautiful lotus eyed personality of Godhead Sri Krishna, who is all pure, you become purified from within and without. So on special days like Janmashtami, Ram Navami - on these auspicious days, we should try as far as possible to put everything else aside, except the glorification, directly of the name and the glories of the lord. And therefore, we refrain from doing anything else except Kirtan, Bhajan, Pooja, Sadhu Satsang and like that. Like Maharaja Parikshit, we don't want any distractions - when will I eat? When you eat you become...you have to rest, you have to digest, you don't…no digest…distract…distractions, you simply want to focus all your attention on hearing and chanting the glories of the lord.

Ekadashi, the eleventh day of the new moon and the full moon, is astrologically a very auspicious time to make spiritual advancement. So on these days also, it is recommended to minimize our bodily necessities and maximize the process of hearing and chanting the glories of the lord.

[devotee:] There are different levels of fast, as we say, some people only fruits and milk, some people only take water, some people don't take water also and some people just refrain from grains. So how do...how do we fit ourself in this kind of standards? How does a neo…a new coming devotee fit himself in these standards?

[Maharaja:] However we can best serve. It depends on our particular service.

Fasting should be as per our capacity and our service

It's like, for instance, Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati Thakur - his disciples, the brahmacharis and the sannyasis, they would perform Nirjala Ekadashi. They would not take any water or any food for the twenty-four hours from sunrise to sunrise. Now there was one brahmachari, one…he had a very important preaching program, that he was to attend, and deliver a lecture and answer questions. It was in the evening and he was feeling very weak. So he approached his Guru Maharaj Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati Thakur, and said that, “Tonight you will have to send someone else to this preaching program, because I am just too weak; because I'm a little sickly and I will not be able to…to…perform good in my preaching tonight.” Then Bhakti Siddhant said, “Then you eat. [Maharaja laughs] We are not attached to the fasting, we are attached to the service.”

Whatever best way you are most fit to serve, you do like that. If you can fast, and at the same time be fully strong to serve, then you can fast. That is very purifying. To…to…Umm…to restrain the senses from sense gratification is very purifying to the heart. But at the same time, the whole purpose of restraining our senses from sense gratification - is to engage our senses fully in God’s service. If fasting interferes with our service, then the result is counterproductive to our spiritual advancement.

You see, we are not tapasvis. We are not simply performing tapasya for the sake of tapasya. We are performing tapasya only for the purpose of increasing our service to the lord. So very strict fast is no water and no food whatsoever. Also, in the strict sense - on Ekadashi you're not supposed to pass urine, you're not supposed to pass stool, you're not supposed to shave, you're not supposed to cut your nails, you're not supposed to decorate your body, you're not supposed to do anything exc…you are not supposed to sleep! This is the strict observance of the Ekadashi - from sunrise to sunrise you do nothing but chant the names of God. Then read up and read the scriptures. You're not supposed to work either. That's strict Ekadashi…nothing in of your...Nothing in your body, nothing out of your body - except the holy name. Huh.

Now if you cannot do that, then at least don't eat or drink; and chant the holy names. If you can't do that, then you just drink water. If you can't do that, then you just eat fruit. If you can't do that, then minimum…Ahh…not minimum, if you can't do that - then once a day only, you eat food but no grains. If you can't do that - then twice a day but no grains. If you can't do that, well, three times a day [Maharaja laughs] but no grains. If you can’t do that, then just don't eat grains. And chant...[Maharaja laughs] and chant the holy names. So, depending on your level of capacity to perform tapasya, and according to the particular service you're expected to render, then you should fast accordingly. We are not so concerned with very…very strict fasts. We are more concerned with the understanding; and apply the basic principle which underlies the reason for fasting - and that is to increase our devotion, our service to God; and minimize our need for sense gratification, bodily maintenance.

Sometimes fasting makes one proud and hard-hearted. Ha! Just like I was speaking to devotees once, that devotees - they do not eat grains. But sometimes the devotee is performing Nirjala Ekadashi, and other devotees they're just not eating grains; once a day they eat just like…fruit and other such foods. So sometimes the person who's performing Nirjala Ekadashi, he is looking at the others who are eating; and saying, “Look at these pigs stuffing their faces with all this food on Ekadashi! [laughter] They are not very advanced! Look at how advanced I am! I'm not eating anything, I am better than them!” It's better to sit down with them and eat, than to think like that. Ha! Because pride is worse than eating! Ha! [laughter]

If you can remain very humble and think ‘My God! These devotees, they are so advanced - that they can eat and still make great spiritual advancement! And me, I am so sinful - if I try to do that, I am…I’ll go to hell! So I am so much lower than all of them, I’d better fast! They are very exalted!’ If you can be like that, then you should fast. But if you're thinking ‘Just see! I am more spiritually advanced than any of these people!’ Then better you eat than become puffed up. Ha!

Any other questions?

We have to replace these sinful thoughts with positive spiritual thoughts for purification

[devotee:] Is it the thought of even eating…suppose we like some particular dish, and we remember that dish on the day of Ekadashi; and we think, ‘Oh my god!’. Eating like that. So does it…is it considered as breaking of the fast - by that thought, that desire?

[Maharaja:]In kali-yuga you...you are not responsible for your thoughts. You are only responsible for your words and your actions. In previous yugas, the thought was non-different than the act. Because in previous yugas, people had such control of their minds - to think about sense gratification, you have to suffer the sinful reaction of doing it. Huh! But in Kali Yuga, your mind is so uncontrolled and wild, that God knows that we'll all go to hell instantly if he keeps this high standard. So therefore, Krishna in Kaliyuga, has given many concessions. Concession is - whatever you think you're not responsible, there's no karmic reaction, or whatever you say or do you…you are. But, if you continuously think on something, by contemplating the objects of the senses; Krishna says dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣūpajāyate [BG 2.62] By contemplating the objects of the senses, one develops attachment for them. From such attachment, lust. From lust, anger - when it's unfulfilled; and through this process we gradually engage in sinful acts. So therefore, a person should be very aware that, although the thought may come to his mind, with his intelligence he has to replace that thought with positive spiritual thoughts. By chanting the names of God, by engaging the mind in positive service to God, by reading the scriptures. Huh!

These are ways that we can replace these sinful thoughts with positive spiritual thoughts for purification. If you're not willing to do that, if we continue to contemplate sense gratification - in due course of time, the mind will force us to engage in the physical acts of these sinful activities. Huh! So when you are thinking like that, you should use your intelligence and withdraw your mind from that thought, and fix it on Krishna - by any means. Huh!

Any other question?

[devotee:][inaudible 00:16:42 to 00:16:48] Why we declare positions, this Kuvera, Narayana and other demigods, is the pleasure for demigods, also we have seen Kuvera next to Badri Narayana, there is Badri [inaudible 00:16:53 to 00:16:55] so who is he and what is the position?

[Maharaja:] Kuvera is a demigod who is in charge of the treasury of the demigods; and in Badrikashram there's a story, of how…don’t know the specific story, you may know it. He personally came down to assist the lord in his leela at that place. It’s not that he has necessarily a special relationship with Narayan, more than other demigods. But in this particular leela, he assisted Badri, Nar Narayan Rishi. Badri Narayan. Like that.

Just like in Tirupati. In Tirupati the story is that Shrinivas was living - the same way as Badri Narayana - he was living like a sadhu, like a hermit. Without anything, simply meditating and…Ha! Lakshmi Devi came to him and then they… they were to have marriage; and of course, he had no money for marriage. So Kuvera being the treasurer of the demigods, he asked him to help provide the finances for a nice marriage. Huh! So Kuvera provided, and then he made a vow. He said that, “I vow that, I…in this particular incarnation, I don't have anything to repay you. But from now on, my devotees - whenever they come to my temple, the Venkateshwara temple; they will give the best things they have, to pay this dowry that I owe!” Huh! So therefore…Umm…every devotee of Venkateshwara is obliged by this vow - to give the best wealth they have; to help the lord of their life pay the debt that he has to Kuvera, until the end of Kali Yuga. The debt is like this - till the end of Kali Yuga, all my devotees will give the best they have, to pay you off. In this way, Kuvera has a very special relationship in this sense. That when the lord appears as Badri Narayan, or when he appears as Venkateshwara; as a very poor sadhu where he has no money, Kuvera comes to his supplies. You see, he has all the assets; of the treasures of the heavens! [Maharaja laughs]

Purpose of shaving one’s head

[devotee:] What’s the importance of shaving of the head and [inaudible 00:19:46 to 00:19:52]

[Maharaja:] Prabhupada: when he was asked, “Why do you shave your head?” he would answer in many different ways. Sometimes he would say, “Because it's very clean.” Hair is a very dirty thing! Huh! You know matajis, you know how it is to wash your hair and dry your hair. Very time consuming. I have personal experience. When I was living as a Sadhu in India; my hair was long - passed my waist, and it was matted. And I would take my bath and it would just be like [Maharaja makes splashing sound] You know just…no towel…you just go [Maharaja makes splashing sound] And it's just like wet and dirty! Huh! And to wash it is very difficult.

And then when I shaved my head, the first thing is…when I took a bath, I couldn't believe how easy it was! [laughter] I am seeing…My God! This is like…baas…finished! [Maharaja Iaughs] I was thinking how clean, how simple, how easy, how nice! So cleanliness is next to godliness, so the shaving your head is very clean.

Another thing is it's very simple, in the sense that - in this day and age, people are very proud of their hair. Huh! I mean…My God…it's one of the biggest industries in the…in the world! Hair fashions! There is male hair fashions, women's hair fashions. In America there's beauty parlors. Every woman with any amount of money, goes to the beauty parlor once a week; and gets certain fashion designs of her hair. Huh! And men - they have certain styles that they have. They go to the barber shop to have; they spend a lot of money like this too. Because they're all wanting to look very beautiful; and they're all competing who can look beau...more beautiful, through the way they comb their hair. Huh! So when you shave your head, you remove yourself from all competition! [laughter] Huh!

It is also said that hair is a...is a…very much a sign of a person's pride. Because the person is very proud of his hair. When you shave your head, it's a sign of cutting off all your pride. So that is another reason. Another reason is it's a sign of renunciation of one’s pride. It’s a sign of renunciation.

Umm…one time, one lady - she was a newspaper reporter - and she was...she had very stylish hair, and she was wearing what they call miniskirt. You have heard? Very short dress, and she had very pretty legs. So she was a newspaper reporter from…I think Newsweek magazine. So she asked Prabhupada, “Why do you shave your head?” He…she said it very challenging. Because…women…in America, that you have this women's liberation. That they don't like anything that men do, that women don't do. [laughter] Huh!

{devotee:] Maharaja, in India also!

[Maharaja:] It's like…you're doing this; why women don't do it, but you do it in your movement. There must be some male chauvinism behind this. So she was very challenging. She said, “Swamiji, why do you shave your head?” And Prabhupada looked at her and said, “Why do you shave your legs?” [laughter] and she was speechless! She didn't know what to say, and then Prabhupada said, “Because to understand God, you have to have a cool head.” Then he said, “Better to have a cool head than coo…cold legs!” [laughter]

Purpose of shaving a child’s head

[devotee:] Maharaj, it’s a practice and our custom; that at the end of one year of the birth, the first birthday, they…they cut the hair of the small kid. So is that how in any way, the pride is coming into the kid? When he doesn’t know what exactly pride is.

[Maharaja:] That is… different… purpose. Shaving a head of the child, there are many reasons. Amongst these many reasons, one is… it is a samskara. By which the parent is offering that child as a sacrifice to God. Another words, sacrifice doesn't mean you have to kill the child. If you sacrifice something of the child to God, and that is the hair. Huh! So it's a purificatory act, that's purifying for both the parents and the child. With the child is undergoing this sacrifice, and the parents are also willing to offer their child as a sacrifice.

Two is, it is said when you cut the child's hair at a young age, it grows better; much more fully rich. Huh! In India today, most people cut their child’s hair just because they want their hair to be more beautiful in the future. But actually it's meant to be a sacrifice, and that sacrifice is a meditation on how actually our body, our mind, our soul, our words - everything is the property of God. This…the ceremony is really meant to be meditated upon that – yes, I belong to God and my child belongs [break 25:43] and the child should understand and I should understand that - really life is God's property. This is just a token gesture of…of accepting this truth - that everything is the property of God and everything of ourselves is the property of God. [indistinct 00:26:02 to 00:26:04]

Purpose of shaving the head when somebody dies

[devotee:] Even when your father dies, or…usually, there is a custom here in India, that you shave up. What is the reason behind it?

[Maharaja:] Principle is the same, is that...that particular time, it's supposed to be a time of renounce…you are supposed to be feeling a sense of renunciation. A sense of devotion to God at that time. Huh! Shaving of the head is…is…is…a…is a…gesture toward God of renunciation. So when the father dies, it's a time where we should feel a great sense of renunciation toward God. Other ways, we should be accepting the fact that ‘God! This father of mine, he is your son. You have given me this father, you can take away this father. It is according to your sweet will. I am your servant.’ Huh! This is a renounced spirit, to accept whatever God offers as his mercy. Therefore the…the external manifestation, that you're taking things in this spirit; is you make this gesture of renunciation, of shaving head.

Now there are many social reasons for doing it. But this is really the essential, real purpose of doing it. At the time of the death of a father or a loved one, it should give us realizations of the temporary nature of this body; and the realization of the supreme authority of God in our life. Huh! He gives life and he takes life, and at this time, where it's actually a very very deep, and very very moving experience of our emotions within our life, it should cause us to become more and more renounced, in the spirit of devotion to God. Huh! So we should take it in that light to make great spiritual advancement.

But if we just do it because it's a custom, there's no spiritual advancement. Simply shaving your head when your father dies because it's the custom, you make no spiritual advancement. The purpose of shaving your head is to make spiritual advancement. But you have to have a philosophical understanding why you're doing it, then you do make spiritual advancement. You do contemplate on these truths, while you're having your head shaved. You will see yourself with head...with your shaved head, when the others see you with the head shaved; everyone is meditating…should be meditating on this philosophical truth of life. Huh!

Real purpose of going on a pilgrimage is to associate with holy men

[devotee:] It is said that you go to a pilgrimage with the pure devotee; that is we want to go to a holy place, we have to go to the pure devotee, is it…is it always that you have to go with a pure devotee or can we go without a pure devotee?

[Maharaja:] You can do as you like. But you will not unders…the holy place can only be revealed to you, through the mercy of the great souls. You can only scratch the external service…surface of the holy places of pilgrimage on your own. But it is the great devotees of the lord who...who bring life, to extract the essence of that holy place and make it visible to you. Huh! Therefore it is very important.

It is said in Srimad-Bhagavatam: in Kali yuga, instead of going to the holy places to associate with holy people, people go simply to take bath. Simply by taking a bath, there is some purification, no doubt. But by associating with holy men, who can reveal the real essence of the dham to you. That is how you can really take full advantage of that Tirth Yatra. You see, our tendency is to see things materially. Huh! But the holy men, they…by their words and by their example, they teach us how to see the real spiritual significance of the Holy Dham. Huh! That is very important.

Also going to a place of pilgrimage is like a ritual. But when you are with a holy man, he actually internalizes that whole experience; and makes it more than a ritual. But he makes it a very sublime means of purifying your heart and deepening your faith and your love.

[devotee:] Why I asked you this question was, I have bought this ticket. Going to Tirupati to shave my kid’s head, so is it really essential to go or…

[Maharaja:] It is a good thing to go. But…but…but then you should understand it's a…it's a ritual. But that ritual is to have…you have the understanding now, then you can go there with the proper understanding. If you take my words with you, then you're really going with a devotee. Huh! [Maharaja laughs]

Live your life completely under the guidance of a Guru

[devotee:] It’s very difficult to find a pure devotee when you go to a holy place. How to look out for a devotee, if you are going to associate with? You said that he should go and associate with him at that place. If he may not go, he may not be, [inaudible 00:32:17 to 00:32:18] there, but there you can look out for a devotee.

[Maharaja:] You see, previously it was very easy. But these days, there are so many people, and so many…it's hard to understand who is really a great soul. That is why it's best to live your life completely under the guidance of a Guru. So you can understand who is a great soul, and when to go to holy places and how to go to holy places. Huh!

Everything belongs to Krishna

[devotee:] Maharaja, that day Sunday, you told about, that Krishna may take up your routine ritual, very dear to you and…allowing your attachment. So what we have to take from it, if you lose your loved ones [inaudible 00:33:02 to 00:33:03] space marking that you are losing, [inaudible 00:33:04 to 00:33:14] what do I lose?

[Maharaja:] What do you have to lose? Nothing is yours! Everything belongs to God. Nothing is yours. To think anything is yours is an illusion. Correct! Do you think your business is yours? It belongs to God. If Krishna wants it back, why should you reject? Huh!

When you surrender to Krishna, you're no longer under the laws of karma.

[devotee:] If I worked hard for that business like, if I had not worked hard, I wouldn’t have had that.

[Maharaja:] Worked hard...if somebody works hard to rob a bank, and gains all that money; and then the government gets…catches him and takes it back. [laughter] Then he asks, “Oh well, I worked so hard for this money! Why are you taking it away from me?” It was never yours rascal! You stole it! [Maharaja laughs] What is the difference? You can work hard but it's Krishna’s. If he wants to take it, it's his to take. It's not yours to complain. Right? If you think it's yours, then you have stolen it.

[devotee:] So we have to look after it, right? We have to look after, whenever we have that thing…maybe wealth…

[Maharaja:] We have to meditate. We have to have a spiritual discipline in our life, by which we can realize the truth - that God is the proprietor of everything and everything is meant for his enjoyment. By associating with saintly persons, by hearing from them, by hearing the scriptures, by chanting the holy names, and by working in this spirit of devotion; we gradually come to the realization of how we are the eternal servants of God. We are meant for his enjoyment and everything we have is meant for his enjoyment. The great souls have prayed,

mārobi rākhobi-jo icchā tohārā
nitya-dāsa prati tuwā adhikārā

[Manasa Deho Geho Jo Kichu Mor 3]

My lord! If you want, you can kill me! Or if you like, you can protect me. If you like, you can give me everything. If you like, you can take everything away! I'm your true…eternal servant. You have every right to deal with me, in any way you please! Huh! This is the attitude of a…of a…of a serious devotee. Now this is from a very high level of thinking. From a more relative level of thinking, we can understand that everything that comes and everything that is taken is due to our previous karma. Huh! That is a fact also, in the worldly sense, that due to our previous good karma…you see, some people - two people are working just as hard; and one is a millionaire and one is still a pauper in the street! It's not simply by your work that you get the results, but it's according to the destiny that you have created in your previous lives. Huh!

So due to some sinful act, you may be losing everything that you have now! That is your own…that is a reaction of what you have done in the past. But you see God is the supreme controller; and the laws of karma are ultimately working under his direction. So he's fair, he's just. So we should rise above this karmic way of thinking and realize that ultimately everything is the mercy of God. Huh! Then we can be satisfied, we can be peaceful whether everything is given or everything is taken. Huh!

You see, God will do whatever is required, when you…when you become God's devotee. Then he will deal with you in whatever way is best for you to advance. If your riches is an impediment…if your attachment to your riches is an impediment to your spiritual growth, then Krishna will start taking things away. But if it's not an impediment, if actually you are seeing it in a proper perspective and using it for God's service; then Krishna will give you much more. There is no need to take it away. He will do whatever is best for your spiritual growth. When you surrender to Krishna, you're no longer under the laws of karma. Your…whatever happens to you is what you need for your spiritual growth. Huh! You don't surrender to Krishna - then you're simply…everything is the law of action and reaction. You gain and you lose according to your past deeds. Huh!

You're going to lose everything anyway to…today or tomorrow. Whenever that day comes, are you going to like it any better? Huh! But if we take in the proper spirit of consciousness, we become elevated, we become purified through that experience. But if we take it in the wrong way, if we take it in the material way - why is this happening to me? This isn't fair! Then you simply cheat yourself of a valuable opportunity to make spiritual advancement; and it's going to happen anyway tomorrow, if it's not happening today; and you're not going to like it any better then. Right? So why not accept the inevita…inevitable, for your spiritual advancement. Rather than fighting against the inevitable - which is a losing battle! Huh! [Maharaja laughs]

The only real positive pleasure is on the spiritual platform

[devotee:] Maharaj, it is very difficult to understand that this is Krishna mercy. We have been used to enjoy our material life and sense pleasures, and suddenly we chant the holy name of Krishna; and we suffer difficulties, and it's very difficult to understand, that this is the mercy of the lord.

[Maharaja:] This is all nonsense! You think the people who are not chanting Hare Krishna are not suffering? In this world, everyone is suffering - today or tomorrow. At the best, there's a small gap of happiness between the suffering. Right? Birth is suffering. Is it not? What does the child do when he comes out of the womb? Does he laugh? You are doctors. Is there ever been in history a time, when the child comes out laughing? One hundred percent of the time, does the child ever come out silent? Like…Umm! Child comes out, and as soon as he realizes what's going on, it starts crying. Correct? So you come to this world crying! Huh! At the time…and when people…when the loved ones that… that you have so much attachment to - when they die, you cry; and when there's pain to your body, you cry. At the time of death, when everything's ripped away - you cry. Right? So this world is a place of crying! Between tears there's this…this…little small gaps of sense of pleasure.

Prabhupada said, “The pleasure of this world is really not pleasure. It's just the absence of pain for some time.” The example he gives that, in the old days when a king wanted to punish someone, he would take very strong executioners; and bring that…that criminal to the river. They would hold them by the hair and they would stick him under the water; and they would hold him under the water, so he couldn't breathe! They would hold him, and hold him, he would be struggling, but he couldn't move; until his...his...he was about…his lungs were about to burst! His head was about to burst! He was about to die of suffocation! He was just meditating - the only thing he wanted in life was a breath of air. Then the executioner - at that very critical moment, he would lift his head up! The person would breathe in, and it will be…Ahh! How beautiful! How wonderful is this bliss…bliss…ananda...ananda; and then a few minutes later [Maharaja makes splashing sound] he ducks him down again, it’s the same thing. So that's the way this material world is! There's so much suffering, so much pain! But every now and then, there's some relief; and we think ‘Aah! How beautiful!’

What is the…considered the most highest form of pleasure in this world? In the western world especially, it's considered sex life. It's…considering that there's almost seven hundred million people in India, this sense must also be similar here. [Maharaja laughs] Population development is not so much, according to very systematic planning of families; but it's usually because it feels so good, you want to do it! Right? But what is that good feeling? It's not a pleasure, it's just the…it's just the relief of a pain. The fact is lusty desires is painful. Huh! It's just driving you crazy, and you just have to get some release from it; and the release is that sexual contact; and really it's…it's just a temporary relief of the pain, of the anxiety, of that desire for sex.

Prahlad Maharaja and Srimad-Bhagavatam says, that sex life is simply scratching an itch, and nothing more than that. Huh! You have an itch and it's driving you crazy…did you ever have an itch? When you scratch it, it feels so good. You're just like [Maharaja makes moaning sound] Right? It's the same way. What I…the sound I make is the same sound people make when they have sex. Right? You just scratch like this, it feels…but actually it's not a good feeling. It's just a relief of a horrible feeling. Actually that's all sense gratification is.

On every level, our…our minds are agitated, because we want to eat something. We want it to taste good, and we're agitated like anything, because we want that; and our tongue is just going [Maharaja makes a sound] Right? Then we eat it, and we think it's really a pleasure to eat it. But actually it's not the pleasure of eating it - it's the relief of the anxiety that you had wanting it. And like… like any other pleasure in this world, that relief is very temporary. There's a little gap of…of relief, and then again you're agitated; because you want more, and that agitation is painful. Correct? So that's the nature of this world, it's a place of suffering; and sometimes there's a relief of the suffering, and that's called pleasure.

So really according to Prahlad and the great souls - there's really no real positive pleasure in this world. The only real positive pleasure is on the spiritual platform - the platform of the soul. The soul’s nature is pleasure, there is no pain. The soul's nature is to love. Love is real pleasure, but that love is eternal. It is not the kind of love that simply bears the fruit of pain in due course of time. All the love for the material objects of this world bears the fruit of pain in due course of time. Is it not? Either death, or before, great sorrow takes place.

We should avoid the tendency to find faults

[devotee:] We see that pure devotees of Guru Maharaja [inaudible 00:45:22 to 00:45:23] many disciples and the disciples have many faults. But we never find Guru Maharaj all time yelling. We don't find Guru Maharaj at them, all time, yelling at a disciple that - you have this fault! You have this fault! He is all time merciful, calm and quiet, nicely telling the disciple. We find ourselves - our tolerance is so low, that our…we cannot tolerate, some even, mild nonsense also. So how to come over this tendency of all time yelling and criticizing. [inaudible 00:45:53 to 00:45:54]

[Maharaja:] The reason why you cannot [No audio 00:45:55 to 00:46:01] The reason one cannot tolerate the fault in another, is because one cannot tolerate that same fault in oneself; and you don't want to admit it in yourself. But when you see it in another, you hate it! It's really something in yours…inside of yourself that you hate, it's not something in that person. Bhakti Siddhanta Sarasvati said, “The only reason why I find faults in others - is because I am honeycombed with five hundred thousand faults myself!” What you hate most about yourself, but because of your false ego you don't want to admit; then you see that in another - you hate it, you despise it, you can't tolerate it!

But you see, by blaming that person or criticizing that person there's no benefit. You only implicate yourself in offense and sinful karma. So therefore when we see the faults in another, we should become introspective, and think ‘it's my impurities that can't tolerate that. If I was pure, I would love this person. I would be able to tolerate whatever injustice he's… he's performing upon me. But because of my impurities in my own heart, I cannot tolerate it; so there's nothing wrong with him, the problem is with me.’

It's like…it is said, “Every time you point one finger at one person, at your enemy, there's three fingers pointing towards yourself. Which means - you should evaluate your own faults at least three times, for every time you evaluate the faults of another. Huh! But because of our false ego, we don't want to admit any faults among...within ourselves. We want to blame everyone else for everything.

But a devotee and [of] the Lord, he is not like that. Jesus Christ said, “Before pointing out the speck in another person's eye, you should take the mountain out of your own eye!” Huh! It's also said in the Bible, “Judge not lest ye be judged.” God is the judge, not us! Let God judge the other person, not us. We should try to make ourselves fit, before we have to go before the judge. [Maharaja laughs] Right?

Fault finding is a disease

[devotee:] So how to make it possible?

[Maharaja:] Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu said amāninā māna-dena kīrtanīyaḥ sadā hariḥ [CC Adi 17.31] A sincere devotee offers all respects to others and expects no respect for oneself. If you expect respect from [for] yourself that means…to the degree you want respect from [for] yourself, to that degree you're going to find faults in others too. To the degree you want no respect for yourself, to that degree you're not going to see any faults in others. Huh!

So finding faults in ot…fault finding is a sign of a very very serious disease of false ego within our heart. And the…the only way to rid ourselves of that false ego, is through the process of purification; which takes place by chanting the holy names of God. And while we're chanting the holy names of God with our intelligence, we should check our lower self from engaging in these sinful acts of fault finding; and we should be always seeking the faults within ourself and try to purify them and always trying to see the good qualities in others. Huh! That is an austerity. It is an austerity of the mind which should be accepted.

In this material world we are anxious to find the faults in others and point them out to the world; and point out to the world our own glories. Huh! But a sincere person on the spiritual path he's very anxious to find the glories of others and point them out to the world; and he's always anxious to admit his own faults to the world. In the eyes of God, that is a great man.

Be in the spirit of servant of whoever we are trying to help

[devotee:] So sometimes criticism for the benefit of the other person. How do we differentiate between these two? Whether we are criticizing for the benefit of another person, or whether we are yelling out of false pride.

[Maharaja:] You have to very carefully evaluate - what are your motives? The next question is - are you in a position to improve that person by pointing out his faults? Will he actually listen to you and benefit? Huh! If it's just going to create antagonism, it's better to just be silent; and by your own example - by your own humble example, try to teach him. Right?

Sometimes if you tell a person his faults, he just reacts very violently in his own mind! He becomes…he doesn't take it positively, but you just ruin your relationship with him. So what is the use? You accept what is constructive. If you want to help the person, you do what's helpful. So to such a person it's best…by your own example, to act so humble - that that person feels ashamed. Huh! Without saying anything against him. If you are in a position, where he's will…where he will listen to you, then you can point out the faults. But you are doing it in a constructive way, to help that person, not to…not to put yourself on a higher position. You should do it in a…in a spirit that you are his servant.

Just like sometimes a mother chastises the child, but she feels like the servant - she's doing it to help the child. She's not doing it - so that the world thinks the mother is better than the child. Alright? So we should be in the spirit of the servant of whoever we're trying to help; and we should accept whatever is favorable and help me in serving that person.

Difference between reading and hearing

[devotee:] Maharaj! Prabhupada said in the first canto Bhagavatam - in the preface that, at the end of reading of these nine chapters…the nine cantos of Srimad Bhagavatam, person will surely become God realized soul. So we couldn’t find it…so where is the problem?

[Maharaja:] Problem is you're not really hearing it![Maharaja laughs]. If you really hear what your…what's being said, you will be a God-realized soul. But if we read it inattentively, that result will not be there. There will certainly be purification. But the complete purification takes place according to the degree that we are attentive with what we are reading; and we are applying the instructions to our life step by step. Huh!

What does it mean by hearing? There's hearing and there's listening. Listening means - it goes in one ear and out the other. Hearing means - it goes in one ear; and it makes such an impact in your life, that you'll just do…you'll sacrifice anything to live according to what you've heard! Huh! That's hearing. That means the instructions of this Srimad-Bhagavatam, when you hear it with such gravity and sincerity that - everything you hear, it changes your life and you apply that principle to your life - then you've heard the whole nine cantos. When you just read it, and it just goes in one ear, and a little may remain there; but most of it goes out the other ear, you haven't really heard. You haven't really assimilated, you haven't really practiced what's being said, and you haven't really heard. Huh!

The Bhagavatam is insisting you have to give up all sense gratification. You have to be completely durant...depend on God's mercy. That was this…that was the lesson that Prahlad Maharaja taught. Is it not? Now have you heard that? If you've heard it, you're doing it. If you've just listened to it, then you've read it, but you're still going on as…as before. Huh! So there's a difference between reading it and hearing it. Hearing means assimilating.

Be serious about your vows

[devotee:] Maharaja! It is said, in Kali, the thoughts…you are not responsible for the thoughts; and you can ask the bad thoughts. While in Bhagavatam introduction, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu’s early disciple had some lusty thoughts. He condemned him, and finally that disciple had to commit suicide. So thinking is so bad?

[Maharaja:] But it was not just his thoughts. You see, this was a very special lesson. Actually that person was a pure devotee! Huh! But the lord, in order to teach those in the renounced order of life how serious their vow is, he wanted to make a very very very extreme example out of this devotee. Huh! Because it’s said, the reaction was…after this took place, none of his devotees would even consider sense gratification in their dreams. Right? [Maharaja laughs] But that person, after he committed suicide, what happened? In a spiritual body, he came to Lord Chaitanya. Every night, he would sing beautiful songs about Krishna to him. Huh! So he wasn't just…an ordinary person would become a ghost if they commit suicide. This person in a…in his original spiritual body, he would come and sing to Lord Chaitanya. So that means this person was pure. But Lord Chaitanya was simply using him as a very very extreme example, to show the world how serious these vows are. Huh!

Association with materialistic people only for service

[devotee:] It’s not allowed, [inaudible 00:57:47 to 00:57:56]

[Maharaja:] Traditionally that is the case. For preaching, sometimes there's exceptions to the traditional…Umm…rules and regulations. But the…but the...but the principle, the spirit must be upheld. It's like…Prabhupada would perform marriage ceremonies. A sannyasi is strictly forbidden to perform marriage ceremonies. A sannyasi is strictly forbidden to it...to associate with materialistic people. Huh! [Maharaja laughs] For preaching purposes, Lord Chaitanya - he would…he wouldn't associate with the king. But then again, he allowed the king to disguise himself, and he associated with him. Right? So for preaching purposes sometimes the devotee is willing to take those risks. But he shouldn't take those risks for his sense gratification. But he can take certain risks for the sake of showing compassion to others.

A devotee should feel fortunate rather than proud

[devotee:] There is a little pride in you and you know something, that is [inaudible 00:59:21 to 00:59:22], and quick to know about it, when you look at others, you feel the [inaudible 00:59:25 to 00:59:27] see the way he is wasting his time, I know something, what is that pride?

[Maharaja:] What is that again?

[devotee:] What is the pride? Like when you look at others who are totally materialistic, when I know something, truth. So when I look at them, I have feeling that these people are wasting their time, I am better off, I know something. So, what is that pride?

[Maharaja:] You should feel like that, but you shouldn’t be proud of yourself. You should be proud of the mercy of your Guru, and you should be proud of the mercy of Krishna. That has given you a life in pursuance of truth. Otherwise this human civilization is a civilization of animals! Huh! They're completely wasting their lives without any real concept of the goal of self-realization; and you would be in that same situation without the mercy of Krishna and the sadhus. Huh! So you should feel fortunate. You should not feel proud, you should feel fortunate. You should feel proud of the mercy that has come upon you, and you should be…you should… feel very humbled by the good fortune that has come upon you.

You shouldn't think I'm better than them, you should think by the mercy of my guru, which I'm so proud of, I'm more fortunate than them. Huh! And you shouldn't think I'm better than them, but you should think that, let me give them my fortune! Huh! You should feel, not proud of yourself, but you should feel compassion towards them. You should be so proud of the mercy of guru and Krishna, that you want to share with the world and make everyone fortunate. Huh!

[devotee:] You may be having desires, or you may hanker for something. You may feel that ‘well, I may use these for God’s worship.’ How you know that.. that is truth? I may be using it, I may…if I get it, I may not use it. I may hanker for something now, which I may say, “Okay, I will use it for God. I am hankering only for that.”

[Maharaja:] First of all, the association of advanced devotees can help us clarify, what really we should aspire for in life; and second of all, if we are sincere, we will honestly evaluate our motives. Huh! If we are insincere, we will justify our own desires for sense gratification, in the name of God’s service. Huh! It is really a question of your sincerity, and the…and the best sign of your sincerity is - rather than taking it in your own hands to discriminate, you reveal your mind to advanced devotees; who can help you to make the right decisions in life, in regard to what you should aspire for and what you should not aspire for. Huh!

Krishna is pleased only by bhakti

[devotee:] So hankering is always there, it’s very difficult. Like, we are constantly looking at proposals [indistinct 1:02:45 to 1:02:48] to want to have something. But you may look at…well…I will use this for Krishna’s service.

[Maharaja:] But you should not be attached. If you're doing it for Krishna’s pleasure, you should accept that Krishna will give you what he wants you to have. Right? Krishna says patraṁ puṣpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayacchati [BG 9.26] If you offer me a leaf or water, or a little fruit with devotion, I will accept it. You don't have to be a rich man; you don't have to have material facilities to serve Krishna. All you need is a heart of devotion and whatever little you have you offer it.

Now you may want something great, to offer it to Krishna, but you don't require it, if Krishna only wants you to offer a leaf or a flower, and you want to offer a diamond ring. Huh! You should be content that Krishna only wants the leaf and water from you. So why should you want the diamond ring, if Krishna doesn't want it? Krishna wants a leaf from you, why should you want a diamond ring? Right? Krishna wants that diamond ring from you, he will give you the diamond ring to give to you. You should aspire for the service; you should not aspire for the objects of the service. Right?

Now if the spiritual master tells you, “You must get this.” Then you should try for it with all your energy. But just the trying for it is the service, whether you get it or not. Right? [Maharaja laughs] If you never get it, you get the same credit as if you got it, if you try for. Because Krishna simply wants our devotion. He doesn't care about the material thing, the material thing means nothing to him. Krishna only is pleased by bhakti.

And what…when you…how do you worship the Ganges? You take water from the Ganges, and you say, “My dear Ganga Mata! Please accept this offering.” And you drop the water. You think she needs your water? It's her water anyway. There's hundreds of trillions of gallons of water flowing at any given moment in the Ganges; and you're taking one handful from her and offering it back. You think, that she thinks, “Oh! Now I'm satisfied! I have one handful of water!” She is satisfied by the devotion in which you offer it. The water is just a means of expressing your devotion. Right? But it's the devotion that she accepts.

So similarly all wealth is already God's property. You cannot offer anything to him. All you can offer is your devotion, everything else is already his. You're just utilizing…certain objects in this world, as a means of expressing your devotion. But devotion is something internal, it's a state of consciousness. So we should not hanker for anything, except devotion. You should not hanker for anything except service. Huh!

Now you may want something material to offer to Krishna, you may try to get it. But you shouldn't be in anxiety whether you get it or not. You should know that it's just…it's just…the sentiment of love, that I'm trying for it, that Krishna is accepting - whether I get it or not. Right? That's called detachment.

Krishna told Arjuna, “Don't be attached to winning this war! Just be attached to fighting as best you can for me. Success or failures”, he said, “that's up to me, that's for me to decide! It's not up to…for you to decide. You simply have to give every bit of your energy to try; and if you do that, you are…you are going to go back to Godhead. Whether you win or lose makes no diff…”, Krishna says, “It makes no difference to me whether you win or lose! The only difference it makes to me, is how you're trying. What type of devotion you're endeavoring.”

Our desire is the most important thing

[devotee:] Devotion also is so difficult to know, what I am doing may be just a ritual. I am doing it. I am supposed to do this…I am doing it. That feeling is not there.

[Maharaja:] But by performing that ritual with a desire, for that feeling to be there, gradually it will be there. Huh! If you simply perform the ritual for the sake of the ritual, there is very little advancement. But if you're performing the ritual with a deep desire that that feeling comes, then the feeling will come. Huh! If you do not have the desire to love Krishna, you should at least have the desire to desire love...to love Krishna; and if you don't have the desire to desire to love Krishna, you should have the desire to desire to desire to love Krishna. [laughter] How many of us love Krishna? How many of us have the desire to love Krishna? We can have the desire for that desire for the desire to love Krishna. Huh!

[devotee:] For short time it will be there, but you will forget about it.

Why association of devotees is important?

[Maharaja:] But then, you should, then… but then…but then when you…then when you associate with devotees again, you remember. Correct? That's why it's very important to have regular association with the devotees. Correct? [Maharaja laughs]

[devotee:] Association is also so difficult! I mean, we don’t have, you have to do your other work. You have to associate with materialistic also, instead of [indistinct 01:08:27 to 01:08:29]

[Maharaja:] Therefore you have to make a priority in your life - the association of devotees; and whenever the opportunity is there, you should take it. You'll find, if you take it very seriously, that you make time, there are…there are so many little minutes and seconds throughout the day that we waste; and if we just don't waste those, and save them; then we'll have more and more time to associate with devotees. Correct? Krishna's in your heart, he sees, how much you are seriously trying, then he will reciprocate. Everything is based on our desire.

God reveals himself according to how you approach him

It's like…Krishna, when he plays his flute, on the Rasa Poornima evening of the Sharad season, the gopis left their homes, left everything to meet Krishna. But some of the gopis, their fathers, their husbands - they would not allow them to go. They would literally forcibly put them in a room, lock all the doors and lock all the windows; and guard it, so that the gopis could not get out to go with Krishna. Now, while the most of the gopis were dancing with Krishna all night, the other gopis were locked in their rooms. They wanted to go so bad, but they couldn't go. So you know what Krishna did? From within their hearts, he danced with them! [Maharaja laughs] Because their desire was so strong to go.

Now if the Gopi said, “Well, they won't let me go! So I'll just watch television tonight! What can I do?” [laughter] “It’s Krishna's mercy I’m not allowed to go! So let me watch…you know…what’s the latest program.” If you do like that, then Krish...do you think Krishna is going to reveal yourself…himself in your heart? No! He's going to say…let her…he’ll just reveal the television within your heart! [laughter]

God reveals himself according to how you approach him. But those gopis, they didn't want anything. They were simply…in their heart, they wanted Krishna so bad! They were as mu…they were more in the…they were more absorbed in the rasa-lila, than the gopis who were with Krishna! Huh! They were more with Krishna than the gopis who were there! Correct? Because their minds were completely intensified, in thinking of Krishna in separation. So if you really want the association of devotees, but you just…but it's actually impossible; then that desire, in and of itself, Krishna will give you the association from within your heart, just by your desire! Huh! And because of that desire, whatever opportunity you do have to associate with the devotees, you'll maximize every second of it.

Some people take things for granted. Huh! Some people they like…live in the temple, they are with devotees all the time. They…they…during classes are like [Maharaja makes snoring sound] [laughter] ‘I have to sit through this class every day!’ Right? They're not very…very anxious, because they're taking the…the words of the Bhagavatam for granted. But someone who's only allowed to come once a week, all week he's thinking ‘How much I need it! How much I want it! But I just don't have time, but I need it. I want it so bad! I'll have to make time, however possible!’ Then when you do finally get there, you're sitting there like this; and every word, you're just… you're just waiting for the next word to just grasp onto; and just embrace it to the core of your heart, and relish it. Right? Hearing one class a week like that is more beneficial, than sleeping through seven. Right? It is said, “Absence makes the heart grow fonder, and familiarity breeds contempt.” So sometimes Krishna puts you in a situation, just to increase your desire. Because if you don't have that increased desire, you…in that situation - if you have too much, you take it for granted. Huh!

Now if the opportunity provides itself and you don't take it, then Krishna will see ‘Ah! He doesn't want it!’. So whenever the opportunity is there to associate with devotees, you should run full speed ahead, to take that association. Sadhu Satsang. And whatever time you don't have that association, you should just accept that…that is an opportunity to increase the anticipation. So that when you do get the association, you will…you will not take it for granted. You will take full advantage of every wealth of a second. Huh! You will take every word, every syllable to heart. Huh!

Our children will follow our example more than our words

[devotee:] What about children, like, although bringing them also into Krishna Consciousness, so as long as they are with us, we try to impart these teachings, [indistinct 01:14:16 to 01:14:29] if you tell them, they will not understand, because they don’t have that [indistinct 01:14:31 to 01:14:35]

[Maharaja:] The first thing is, by your own example, you have to set the standard you want them to see. Huh! You have to…more than anything in a child's life, child is very influenced by what he sees the parents doing. It's not so much what you tell them, it’s what they see. You have to tell them the right thing. They have to be educated to understand why you're doing what you're doing. So you must instruct them, but what they see will really, either make what you tell them meaningful or meaningless. If you tell them something that they don't see, that you're not doing it yourself, it's meaningless. But if you tell them something, and they see how dedicated and committed you are to doing it; then it’s going to have a very great impact on them. Correct? So that's the first thing.

You see children are very perceptive, more than you think. They might look like stupid little children playing around and just doing all nonsense. But actually they see through you! They see right into your heart! You cannot hide things from your children! You may…intellectually you can hide things. But you cannot hide the impressions on their hearts. If you're a hypocrite, they know you're a hypocrite! You can't hide it! You have to be genuine in your devotion yourself, and they…they will recognize that; and that will have a tremendous impact in their life. So the first thing you have to do is become very strict yourself. If not for your own spiritual advancement, out of your love for your children. You owe it to them. Correct?

And second of all, according to your means, you should try to monitor the environment that your children is entering into in this world. You should try as far as possible to restrict any association of sinful environment. It may not be entirely, but according to your capacity; you should not neglect the needs of your child in this regard. You should be very careful to see who your child is associating with, and at the very be…if you see he's associating with somebody who has a bad influence; before he becomes attached to that person's association, and before he becomes influenced and habituated to those sinful activities, you should cut it at the very beginning, somehow or other; and you should encourage your child to associate with persons who are pious or saintly. Huh! It’s very difficult! That is why I am sannyasi! [Everyone laughs]

You have a very difficult task, but you have to do it, the best you possibly can. Your own example is completely in your own hands. You can set that example - that's the most important service you can render to your child. The second thing is to put the child in a school, where he's going to have the best environment within your means. Huh! Try to help the child select friends, that you know that they are coming from families that are pious. Otherwise if it's a sinful family, he's going to go; and he's going to associate with all kinds of bad influences. So you should be very very careful.///////////1:18:17

[devotee:] If we restrict him, in our homes like…he may be curious about that, so he might try to…you don’t want to, [inaudible 01:18:23 to 01:18:25] he may do like, materialistic. Right? He will feel curious about it. [inaudible 01:18:31 to 01:18:33]

[Maharaja:] He may do like that. But then what happens…you see, even though he may become a little…as he grows older…grows in his teenage years, where he wants to adventure and explore into the nature of sinful life; he will have a very deep impression by his early years of what his parents have given him; and he will very easily be able to see through it in due course of time - see through the fallacy of the promise of maya and these sinful activities. Huh! Maya is simply giving us false promises at every moment, that - if you accept this you will be happy. But no matter how much you accept, you're never happy! Right? [Maharaja laughs].

So if we raise the children properly, they will have the good in…they will have spiritual eyes and integrity in their life; by which they will gradually be able to see through the falsity of the promises of maya. So you have a very very great responsibility, and you have to take it very seriously.

[devotee:] [inaudible 01:19:46 to 01:19:48] questions about admission of our kids, nowadays nothing, there…I mean, for the admission you have to pay a donation or you have to take influence. Without that your son won’t get admission in any good school. Do you think I am doing the right thing in doing that?

We have to do the needful

[Maharaja:] You have to do the needful.

[devotee:] There’s nothing wrong with that?

[Maharaja:] Whatever…whatever it takes to provide the best and most Krishna conscious environment for your child, you should do whatever is necessary. Hmm! You can judge a…you could judge a tree by its fruit or you can judge a thing by its result. Huh! Sometimes the ends justify the means! In this world, sometimes you just have to cheat! As though…but if the… if the reason why you're cheating is to do something not harmful, but good for everyone involved; then…then you should cheat! [Maharaja laughs] It's not really…it's not really cheating. It’s actually a service to God, service to your child. It's the way this world is…this is kali-yuga. Huh!

Just like, sometimes when we want to get books into the country, we have to pay off some customs man. Huh! Some people pay off customs man to get drugs in the country, we pay off a customs man to get spiritual books in the country. That's not really cheating, isn’t it? Huh! Not that we do like that, but some people do like that. [Maharaja laughs]

Any other questions?

Remember the story of Kuvera coming to Badrikashram? Why he came? Do you remember the story? Huh! I don’t remember. I know the principle. [Maharaja laughs] Because Badrika…Badrinath was very poor. He was performing tapasya. Right? He's sitting in a lotus like this and Lakshmi came as a tree, so he was very poor. So Kuvera came because he wasn't like Srinivas in Tirupati, he came to provide wealth and riches. But I am…I don’t remember exactly the story. It’s in…it’s in the book and we should read it.

Any other questions? Thank you very much for your kind patience, sitting through my long ended words.

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
